title: Animal Sounds
description: Create a parent class called Animal with a method speak that returns 'Some sound'. Create two child classes: Dog(Animal) and Cat(Animal) that each override speak(). Dog.speak() must return 'Woof! Woof!' and Cat.speak() must return 'Meow!'. Then create instances dog = Dog() and cat = Cat(), and use print() to output their speak() results, each on its own line.
starter_code:
# Define Animal class and child classes here

# Create instances and print their sounds

code_requirements.description: Creates at least one child class that inherits from a parent
code_requirements.fail_message: Create child classes that inherit from a parent class using the syntax class Child(Parent):

code_requirements.description: Defines a speak method with self as the first parameter
code_requirements.fail_message: Each class needs a speak() method with 'self' as its first parameter.

code_requirements.description: Uses return to send back a string value
code_requirements.fail_message: Use 'return' to send the sound string back from the speak() method instead of printing inside it.

hints: Parent class Animal should have def speak(self): return 'Some sound'
Child classes: class Dog(Animal): with its own def speak(self): method that returns the dog sound
At the end, print(dog.speak()) and print(cat.speak()) each on separate lines
